description: README + user-facing docs rewrite in cockroachdb style. Pre-launch alpha framing throughout. 12 stories: README ≤120 lines + LICENSE (Apache 2.0) add, Concepts section NEW (5 pages), Get Started rewrite, Deploy rewrite, Operate section NEW (physical git mv of 4 best-practices pages + 2 new — drain-cluster, scaling), Best Practices core refresh (placement/tracing/web-ui), Best Practices tail refresh + compliance/billing NEW, Architecture overview + 4 mermaid pages (put-flow/multi-cluster-routing/drain-pipeline/worker-leader-election — renamed to avoid existing workers.md collision), Architecture existing-pages sanitization audit (component names + interface signatures allowed, arbitrary file:line banned), Reference + S3 Compatibility polish, Navigation + sidebar weight reshuffle (architecture 30→60, best-practices 40→30, reference 60→40, adr 45→80) + top-of-docs card grid via existing {{< card >}} shortcode, smoke + grep-gate (zero internal/file:/gocql/goceph/LWT/bucket_stats in user-facing sections) + ROADMAP new-and-closed entry + PRD removal. Mermaid via fenced code blocks (hugo-book ships mermaid.min.js — no hugo.toml change needed). Pre-launch product per [Pre-launch no deploys] memory.

userStories.title: README rewrite + LICENSE add (Apache 2.0)
userStories.description: As a developer evaluating Strata, I want a concise README + actual LICENSE file so I know what Strata is, that it's alpha, what license it ships under, and how to spin it up — without diving into Cassandra internals on first scroll.
acceptanceCriteria: **NEW**: Add `LICENSE` file at repo root with Apache 2.0 canonical text (from https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0.txt — copyright `Strata authors`, year `2024-`).
README.md rewritten to ≤120 lines.

Section order: (a) Title + CI badge + license badge (`![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0-blue)`); (b) Alpha banner `> ⚠️ **Alpha software.** Pre-launch; no production deploys. APIs and schemas may change without notice.`; (c) One-line tagline `S3-compatible object gateway — drop-in replacement for Ceph RGW. Metadata in Cassandra or TiKV; data in RADOS, S3, or memory.`; (d) What is Strata — 3-5 sentences without internals; (e) Key features — 5-7 user-language bullets; (f) Features vs RGW table (6-8 rows: bucket index scaling, online resharding, multi-cluster routing, drain ops, observability, admin API, deployment shape, license); (g) Status & maturity — `Strata is in alpha — pre-launch, no production deploys yet. Shipped features below; open work tracked in [ROADMAP.md](ROADMAP.md).` + 3-line Shipped/In flight/Parked summary; (h) Quickstart × 2 (memory + TiKV stack); (i) Documentation link; (j) License one-line `Apache 2.0 — see [LICENSE](LICENSE).`
**Removed**: phase status implementation table; gocql/LWT/goceph jargon; metadata-plane/data-plane internals paragraph; arbitrary file:line references; Phase X mentions.
Cockroachdb-style prose: short paragraphs, scannable bullets, no embedded code reading.
`make docs-build` green (verify no broken links to docs site URLs).
License badge URL renders (img.shields.io canonical badge service).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Concepts section NEW
userStories.description: As a new user reading the docs, I want a Concepts section explaining what Strata is, S3 surface, multi-cluster, drain, workers — user-language with an architecture-overview mermaid diagram.
acceptanceCriteria: New section `docs/site/content/concepts/_index.md` (weight: 15, bookFlatSection: true, description: `Strata concepts — S3 surface, multi-cluster routing, drain & rebalance, worker model.`).

Pages: (a) `_index.md` — 3-5 sentences What Strata is + architecture overview mermaid diagram (S3 client → Gateway → Metadata [Cassandra/TiKV] + Data [RADOS/S3/memory] + Workers); (b) `s3-surface.md` — what S3 surface Strata speaks, 1-2 paragraphs per major op family (bucket, object, multipart, versioning, ACL, lifecycle, encryption, replication); (c) `multi-cluster.md` — per-bucket placement policies, weighted default routing, EC support — user-facing; (d) `drain-rebalance.md` — drain lifecycle (live → draining → evacuating → removed) plain English, ETA, operator actions, mermaid state-transition diagram; (e) `workers.md` — what workers Strata runs (GC, lifecycle, rebalance, replicator, notify, access-log, inventory, audit-export, manifest-rewriter, usage-rollup) one paragraph each.
**NO `file:line` references**, NO `Cassandra LWT` jargon, NO `goceph` mentions.
Cross-link forward to `/architecture/` for readers wanting impl details.

**Mermaid first-write sanity check**: render via `make docs-serve`, confirm SVG appears (not raw fenced block). Verify hugo-book's `_shortcodes/mermaid.html` + `static/mermaid.min.js` work without hugo.toml change.
`make docs-build` green; all internal links resolve.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Get Started section rewrite
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a clear install + first-bucket walkthrough so I can verify Strata works in <5 minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite `docs/site/content/get-started/_index.md` cockroachdb-style.

Sections: (a) Prerequisites — Docker (for stack) or Go 1.23+ (for source). Macos lima note. (b) Install — `git clone` + `make dev`. Existing `One-command dev` section from ralph/dx-lab US-001 stays. (c) Verify — `curl http://localhost:9999/healthz` → 200; open operator console at `http://localhost:9999/console/` (URL verified — `internal/serverapp/serverapp.go:254` mounts `strataconsole.ConsoleHandler()` at `/console/`). (d) First bucket + first object — `aws s3 mb` + `aws s3 cp` with `--endpoint-url`. (e) What's next — links to Concepts, Deploy, Operate.
Pure user-language: NO `bucket_stats` / `manifest CAS` / `LWT` references.
`make docs-build` green.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Deploy section rewrite
userStories.description: As an operator deploying Strata, I want a straight-line guide for each deploy shape (Docker, Kubernetes, single-node prod) without diving into compose YAML internals.
acceptanceCriteria: Rewrite `docs/site/content/deploy/_index.md` + sub-pages (`docker-compose.md`, `kubernetes.md`, `multi-replica.md`, `single-node.md`) user-first.
**Each sub-page MUST follow same template**: Prerequisites → Install → Configure (top env vars one-line each) → Verify → Monitor → Troubleshoot.
Helm install path documented (from ralph/dx-lab US-003).
**Reference impl details by component name, not file:line**: `the gateway accepts S3 traffic on port 9999` (good); `see internal/s3api/server.go:109` (bad — refactor out).
Cross-link to `/operate/` for ongoing ops + to `/reference/env-vars/` for full env knob table.
`make docs-build` green.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Operate section — PHYSICAL MOVE + new workflows
userStories.description: As an operator running Strata in production-ish mode, I want an Operate section. Built by physically MOVING 4 day-2 ops pages out of `/best-practices/` (not duplicating) + adding 2 new workflow pages.
acceptanceCriteria: New section `docs/site/content/operate/_index.md` (weight: 25, bookFlatSection: true, description: `Day-2 ops workflows — drain a cluster, monitor, scale, back up.`).

**Physical MOVE** via `git mv` (preserve history) from `/best-practices/` → `/operate/`: (a) `backup-restore.md` → `/operate/backup-restore.md`; (b) `monitoring.md` → `/operate/monitoring.md`; (c) `sizing.md` → `/operate/scaling.md` (rename — Scaling reads better in Operate); (d) `capacity-planning.md` → `/operate/capacity-planning.md`.

**Each moved page**: refresh frontmatter (`weight`, `description`); replace remaining arbitrary `file:line` / `internal/...` references with component names; verify cross-links inside the moved page still resolve.

**NEW page** `/operate/drain-cluster.md`: step-by-step drain workflow. POST `/admin/v1/clusters/{id}/drain` → watch progress → wait for deregister-ready → remove from env. Reference live ETA + bandwidth chips from ralph/drain-rebalance-transparency. Mermaid diagram of state transitions.
**NEW** `/operate/_index.md`: section landing — card grid linking 5 sub-pages.

**Cross-link audit**: any docs page that linked to `/best-practices/backup-restore/` / `/monitoring/` / `/sizing/` / `/capacity-planning/` MUST update to `/operate/...`. Grep for old paths after move; zero hits expected.
**NO impl details** (LWT, manifest CAS, leader election) — those stay in Architecture. Reference `the drain pipeline` + link to architecture deep dive.
`make docs-build` green.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

**NO arbitrary `file:line` references**, NO `internal/...` path mentions. Component names like `gateway`, `rebalance worker`, `lifecycle worker` allowed.
`make docs-build` green.

`_index.md` updated with card grid for remaining Best Practices pages (post-move: placement-rebalance, tracing, web-ui, gc-lifecycle-tuning, quotas-billing, s3-multi-cluster, compliance, billing — total 8).

userStories.title: Architecture section overview + 4 new mermaid pages
userStories.description: Rewrite Architecture section landing with component-level overview + mermaid diagram, and add 4 new mermaid-bearing pages for critical flows.
acceptanceCriteria: **Mermaid usage** confirmed: fenced ` ```mermaid ` code block renders to SVG via hugo-book's built-in `_shortcodes/mermaid.html` + `static/mermaid.min.js`. NO `hugo.toml` change needed. Verify with first diagram via `make docs-serve`.

Rewrite `docs/site/content/architecture/_index.md`: 5-paragraph component-level overview + mermaid architecture diagram. List existing 11 sub-pages (`auth`, `backends`, `benchmarks`, `data-backend`, `meta-store`, `migrations`, `observability`, `router`, `sharding`, `storage`, `workers`) + new 4 in card grid.

**4 NEW mermaid-bearing sub-pages**: (a) `architecture/put-flow.md` — Object PUT flow sequenceDiagram: client → gateway → SigV4 verify → meta (CreateObject CAS) → data (chunk PUT) → meta (manifest update). (b) `architecture/multi-cluster-routing.md` — flowchart: placement policy → cluster weight wheel → data backend selector → RADOS/S3/memory. (c) `architecture/drain-pipeline.md` — stateDiagram: live → draining → evacuating → removed; rebalance worker + GC fan-out + deregister-ready gates. (d) `architecture/worker-leader-election.md` — flowchart: supervisor → per-worker leases + heartbeat chip + shard fan-out. **Renamed from 'workers' to avoid collision with existing `architecture/workers.md`**.
**Component names referenced in diagrams MUST match code identifiers** so engineers can grep: e.g. `s3api.Server`, `meta.Store`, `data.Backend`, `leader.Session`, `rebalance.ProgressTracker`.
**No arbitrary `file:line`** in prose of these new pages — component names + interface signatures only.
`make docs-build` green; verify each mermaid SVG renders via `make docs-serve`.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Architecture existing pages — sanitization audit
userStories.description: Audit 11 existing Architecture sub-pages. Replace arbitrary file:line references with component names + interface signatures. Architecture is deep-dive bucket — component refs allowed, arbitrary line numbers banned.
acceptanceCriteria: For each of 11 existing sub-pages (`auth`, `backends`, `benchmarks`, `data-backend`, `meta-store`, `migrations`, `observability`, `router`, `sharding`, `storage`, `workers`), grep `internal/[^"]*\.go:[0-9]+` and `cmd/[^"]*\.go:[0-9]+` patterns. Replace each hit with equivalent component reference: `internal/s3api/server.go:223` → `s3api.Server.handleBucket`; `internal/meta/cassandra/store.go:4013` → `meta.cassandra.Store.UpdateObjectSSEWrap`; etc.

Tables / code listings that genuinely benefit from a line number (e.g. benchmarks pointing at a specific benchmark-table row) MAY keep file:line — case-by-case judgment, log decisions in progress.txt.
**Migrations sub-section** (`/architecture/migrations/`) is historical and intentionally line-anchored — leave untouched.
**`/architecture/benchmarks/`** — only sanitize narrative paragraphs, keep raw bench output verbatim.
`make docs-build` green.
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Smoke validation + ROADMAP new-and-closed + PRD removal
userStories.description: As a future-maintainer, I want the docs-rewrite cycle verified with docs-build + manual-browse + markdown-lint grep + ROADMAP entry + PRD removal.
acceptanceCriteria: Run `make docs-build` → 0 errors, 0 warnings, all pages render. Capture page count delta vs main in progress.txt (today ~75; expected ~+13 from Concepts(5) + Operate(2 new — drain-cluster + scaling rename) + Best Practices(2 new — compliance + billing) + Architecture(4 new mermaid pages); moves are zero-sum so net delta ~+13).

Run `make docs-serve` and manually browse: `/` → `/get-started/` → `/concepts/` → `/deploy/` → `/operate/` → `/best-practices/` → `/reference/` → `/s3-compatibility/` → `/architecture/` → `/developers/` → `/adr/`. Each section `_index.md` renders; sidebar ordering matches US-011 spec. Capture one-line confirmation per section in progress.txt.
**Mermaid sanity check**: open each of 5 mermaid diagrams (1 in concepts/_index, 4 in architecture sub-pages) in `make docs-serve` — they must render to SVG inline (not raw fenced code block).

**Markdown-lint grep gate**: `grep -rE 'internal/[a-z]|file:[0-9]+|gocql|goceph|LWT|bucket_stats' docs/site/content/{get-started,concepts,deploy,operate,best-practices,reference,s3-compatibility}/` → zero matches in user-facing sections. Patterns MAY appear in `/architecture/`, `/developers/`, `/adr/` — those are deep-dive / contributor-facing.
**LICENSE file verified**: `head -3 LICENSE` shows `Apache License` text; `wc -l LICENSE` ≥ 200 lines (full Apache 2.0 text).

**NEW ROADMAP entry** under `## Developer experience` (per CLAUDE.md `Discovering a new gap` rule — docs rewrite not pre-roadmapped). Title: `User-facing README + docs rewrite (cockroachdb-style).` Flipped to Done in same commit. References US-001..US-012 + new Concepts + Operate sections + 5 mermaid diagrams + LICENSE add + sidebar reshuffle. Carries `(commit pending)` placeholder; SHA backfill on main as fast-follow.
`tasks/prd-readme-docs-rewrite.md` REMOVED via `git rm`.
`scripts/ralph/progress.txt` carries one US-012 block summarising docs-build + navigation + markdown-lint grep + LICENSE verification.
`make vet` + `go test -race ./...` green (no Go code touched — vacuous; listed for AC-format consistency).
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
